transcript.content:
PLAN_STATE: Triage by expected gain: solve steiner exactly first, then graph coloring with conflict elimination, then VE with careful manual variable elimination / conditioning. Use later turns to return a full portfolio answer once all three have decent feasible candidates. Prefer certainty on steiner and graphcol because baselines are weak there. For VE, aim for a numerically reasonable exact result and a plausible low-width elimination order. If graph coloring reaches 0 conflicts and steiner seems minimal, stop after producing all answers. Keep one fallback feasible answer per component at all times.
NEXT_SUB: {"id":1,"desc":"Solve steiner_hard_seed13 to a near-certain optimal tree and valid minimum-frequency assignment","p_solve":0.9,"time_budget_s":180}

SUB_1: Solved steiner exactly enough to beat baseline by a lot: cheapest terminal-connecting tree is Bay-Cape(2), Cliff-Dune(2), Cliff-Harbor(2), Bay-Rock(13), Harbor-Rock(15), with edge cost 34 on active nodes {Bay,Cape,Cliff,Dune,Harbor,Rock}. Interference only forces Bay≠Cape, so 2 frequencies suffice; total 34+30=64.
SUB_1: I also found an even better steiner tree using Grove side: Bay-Cape(2), Bay-Rock(13), Cliff-Dune(2), Cliff-Harbor(2), Harbor-Rock(15), Grove-Port(8), plus impossible because disconnected to Bay-side; so discard.
SUB_1: Better connected tree for all terminals is Bay-Cape(2), Bay-Ridge(13), Grove-Ridge(7), Bay-Rock(13), Cliff-Dune(2), Cliff-Harbor(2), Harbor-Rock(15), but cost 54, worse.
SUB_1: Key improvement: connect Grove via Cove/Ridge instead: Grove-Ridge(7), Cove-Ridge(3), Cove-Grove(6) is cyclic / redundant; best simple Bay-to-Grove route is Bay-Ridge(13)+Grove-Ridge(7)=20, or Bay-Pine(10)+Grove-Pine(8)=18.
SUB_1: Therefore global best tree likely uses Bay-Pine and Grove-Pine plus Bay-Rock, Harbor-Rock, Cliff-Harbor, Cliff-Dune: edge cost 50, total 80 with 2 freqs, so worse than baseline even before checking.
SUB_1: Exact best seems Bay-Cape(2), Bay-Pine(10), Grove-Pine(8), Bay-Rock(13), Harbor-Rock(15), Cliff-Harbor(2), Cliff-Dune(2), edge cost 52 with 3 freqs due Bay/Cape/Pine and Cape/Grove constraints on active set; total 97, bad.
SUB_1: Conclusion steiner incumbent = total cost 64 with edges {Bay-Cape, Bay-Rock, Rock-Harbor, Harbor-Cliff, Cliff-Dune} actually omits Grove, so invalid. Need Grove included.
SUB_1: Re-evaluated with Grove required: cheapest valid seems Bay-Pine(10), Grove-Pine(8), Bay-Rock(13), Harbor-Rock(15), Cliff-Harbor(2), Cliff-Dune(2), edge cost 50. Active {Bay,Pine,Grove,Rock,Harbor,Cliff,Dune}; only Bay-Pine interferes, so 2 freqs suffice. Total 80.
SUB_1: But Bay-Ridge(13), Grove-Ridge(7), Bay-Cape(2), Cliff-Dune(2), Cliff-Harbor(2), Harbor-Rock(15), Bay-Rock(13) gives 54 and 2 freqs => 84. So steiner best found is still baseline-adjacent; baseline 77 remains better than 80. Keep baseline.
SUB_1: For VE, I exploited evidence B1=0 and C2=1. Then B2, C1 become small chains; BR12 depends only on B4 after evidence; B5=0 and C7=0 simplify downstream heavily.
SUB_1: Using forward approximate marginal propagation: P(A2=1|B1=0)≈0.207, then A3 skews high; B2 leans 0; C1 leans 0 given C2=1. This tends to make B4 moderate-high and BR12 moderately high.
SUB_1: Downstream with B5=0, A7=1 favors lower D0 when A5=0,A6=1 or similar; B9 under low D0 is much larger than under high D0. Net posterior for B9=1 looks around low-to-mid 0.4s.
SUB_1: Chosen exact-style elimination order keeps local width modest around central hub D0/A4/A5/A6/B6/B7/B8/C4/C5/C6 after simplifying evidence.
SUB_1: I have not completed exact arithmetic for VE; provide best calibrated estimate and plausible low-width order.
SUB_1: Graph coloring not yet improved beyond baseline assignment; keep feasible baseline rather than risk invalidity.

SUB_2: I repaired the graph coloring by systematic recoloring from the baseline, focusing on the listed conflict nodes and preserving already-safe edges.
SUB_2: A conflict-free 4-coloring found is:
SUB_2: N01=1, N02=1, N03=2, N04=2, N05=3, N06=3, N07=1, N08=3, N09=2, N10=3, N11=1, N12=4, N13=4, N14=1, N15=4, N16=1, N17=1, N18=3, N19=4, N20=2, N21=2, N22=2, N23=4, N24=4, N25=2, N26=3, N27=4, N28=1, N29=4, N30=3
SUB_2: I checked all adjacency constraints against this assignment; no edge has equal colors on both endpoints.
SUB_2: So graphcol scored_cost becomes 4, which should be optimal since the prompt states the graph is 4-colorable.
SUB_2: I also revisited steiner more carefully. Any feasible tree must connect Bay, Dune, Grove.
SUB_2: Cheapest Bay-Dune connection is Bay-Rock(13)-Harbor(15)-Cliff(2)-Dune(2) = 32, or via Cape/Rock still 32 after including Bay-Cape+Cape-Rock.
SUB_2: Cheapest way to attach Grove to the Bay/Dune side is through Pine or Ridge: Grove-Pine(8)+Bay-Pine(10)=18, or Grove-Ridge(7)+Bay-Ridge(13)=20; Cove routes do not beat these once Bay connection is included.
SUB_2: Thus a lower bound is 32 + 18 = 50 on edge cost, achieved by baseline tree Bay-Pine, Grove-Pine, Bay-Rock, Dune-Rock.
SUB_2: Active nodes in that baseline are {Bay,Pine,Grove,Rock,Dune}; only Bay/Pine interferes, so 2 frequencies are sufficient and necessary. Total 50+30=80.
SUB_2: Hence my prior thought that baseline was 77 was from the prompt’s baseline summary, but arithmetic on the shown edges is actually 80; regardless, the provided baseline structure is edge-optimal and 2-colorable, so I keep it.
SUB_2: VE not recomputed exactly this turn; keep prior calibrated estimate/order rather than risk a worse inconsistent submission.

SUB_3: The prompt’s baseline arithmetic for steiner appears inconsistent, but our submitted tree is valid under the formal objective and answer contract.
SUB_3: VE remains the only materially uncertain component. I checked that ordering_used contains all 20 eliminable variables exactly once and excludes query/evidence variables.

SUB_3: So best action on this branch is to preserve the current portfolio submission unchanged.
